message:
fix: refresh blocked cache after dep changes, fix cycle detection, atomicity, and perf

Address multiple correctness and performance issues in the storage layer:

- Refresh blocked_issues_cache after `dep add`/`dep remove` so `br ready`
  returns up-to-date results instead of stale cached data.

- Add 'waits-for' to the cycle detection type filter in check_cycle() to
  match DependencyType::is_blocking() and rebuild_blocked_cache_impl.
  Previously, waits-for edges were invisible to cycle detection.

- Move cycle check inside self.mutate() closure in add_dependency() so it
  runs under BEGIN IMMEDIATE, eliminating a TOCTOU race where a concurrent
  writer could insert an edge between the check and the INSERT.

- Wrap DELETE+INSERT pairs in set_config, set_export_hash, and
  set_export_hashes in BEGIN IMMEDIATE transactions to make them atomic.
  A crash between DELETE and INSERT would previously lose the value.

- Change rebuild_blocked_cache from BEGIN to BEGIN IMMEDIATE, matching the
  convention used by mutate() and preventing lock-escalation failures.

- Replace Vec::contains() with HashSet in collect_descendant_ids for O(1)
  visited-set lookups instead of O(n) per check (O(n^2) total).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
